title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 August 2024.

items.title: **Star Wars Jedi: Survivor** is coming to PS4 and Xbox One in September
bullets: September 17th ($49.99)

The PC version of Star Wars Jedi: Survivor will also receive an update in the coming weeks, bringing “enhancements to the game’s technical performance, controls and more, plus a variety of quality-of-life improvements”

items.title: **Fall Guys** is now in **Fortnite**
bullets: For a limited time, you can play Fall Guys within Fortnite's battle royale, via an enormous obstacle course currently floating above the map. Enter into it and your in-game character will be turned into a customised Fall Guy bean.
Outside of Fortnite's battle royale, you can play custom Fall Guys levels made by Mediatonic and additional mini-games made by other creators, who have had access to Fall Guys assets ahead of time.
The tie-up appears permanent, with a dedicated Fall Guys row in Fortnite's main Discover menu, and all non-licensed Fortnite skins given a Fall Guys bean character equivalent.

items.title: Acclaimed biking game **Descenders** is getting an extreme sports sort-of-sequel
bullets: "Descenders Next" - 2025
Announced so far is snowboarding and mountainboarding

items.title: **Dead by Daylight**'s **Castlevania** collaboration adds Dracula and Trevor Belmont later this month
bullets: Dead by Daylight's Castlevania Chapter is available now for public testing on PC, but it'll get its full release across all platforms on 27th August, when it'll cost around $7.99 USD

items.title: **Five Nights at Freddy**’s x **Dead by Daylight** crossover announced for 2025
August’s **PlayStation Plus** Essential games are now live
bullets: Lego Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga (PS4/5)
Five Nights at Freddy’s Security Breach (PS4/5)
Ender Lilies: Quietus of the Knights (PS4)

items.title: **Xbox Game Pass** August line-up announced
bullets: Arriving
Creatures of Ava (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) - 7th August
Crash Bandicoot N. Sane Trilogy (Cloud, Console, and PC) - 8th August
Mafia: Definitive Edition (Cloud, Console, and PC) - 13th August
Leaving (15th August)
Airborne Kingdoms (Cloud, Console, and PC)
Offworld Trading Company (PC)
Shadow Warrior 3 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
The Texas Chain Saw Massacre (Cloud, Console, and PC)

items.title: **PlayStation** Q1 Results
bullets: Q1 Numbers:
PS5 Sales: 2.4 million units, down from 3.3 million last year
Gaming Division Sales: Up 12% year-on-year
Operating Income: Up 32%, reaching ¥65.2 billion ($443 million)
PS5 Sales: 61.7 million units sold to date

items.title: **Square Enix** Q1 Results
bullets: The company's overall net sales dropped by 18.4 percent year-on-year for the most recent quarter.
This is driven in part due to a decline in sales of new games. By comparison, the same quarter in 2023 saw the release of Final Fantasy 16 and the Final Fantasy Pixel Remasters.

items.title: **Warner Bros**. is reportedly considering selling a stake in its video games business
bullets: Warner Bros. Discovery exec JB Perrette said earlier this year that the company’s games business revolves around four main intellectual properties, “which are all billion-dollar plus businesses themselves”.
The four IP are: Mortal Kombat, Game of Thrones, DC and Harry Potter
